info.title: File API
info.description: The File API enables you to upload, store, manage, and share files within the epilot platform. ## Key Features - **Upload files** to temporary storage and save them permanently as File entities - **Generate previews** (thumbnails) for images and documents - **Create public links** to share private files externally - **Organize files** into collections for better management - **Version control** with automatic file versioning on updates ## File Upload Workflow 1. Call `uploadFileV2` to get a pre-signed S3 URL 2. Upload your file directly to S3 using the pre-signed URL (PUT request) 3. Call `saveFileV2` with the S3 reference to create a permanent File entity ## Changelog View API Changelog

tags.name: File
tags.description:
Core file operations for uploading, saving, retrieving, and deleting files.

Files are stored as epilot entities with the `file` schema and support:
- Multiple versions (each save creates a new version)
- Custom metadata and tags
- Relations to other entities (contacts, orders, etc.)
- Access control (private or public-read)

tags.name: Preview
tags.description:
Generate thumbnail previews for files. Supports images, PDFs, and common document formats.

Preview images are generated on-demand and cached for performance.
You can specify custom dimensions using the `w` (width) and `h` (height) parameters.

tags.name: Public Links
tags.description:
Create shareable public links for private files.

Public links allow external users to access files without authentication.
Links are permanent until revoked and include the filename for user-friendly URLs.

tags.name: Session
tags.description: Browser session management for cookie-based authentication. Use `getSession` to convert a Bearer token into a session cookie. This enables: - Direct use of preview URLs in `` tags - File downloads without manual token handling **Typical flow:** 1. Authenticate and obtain a Bearer token 2. Call `GET /v1/files/session` with the Bearer token 3. Subsequent requests use the session cookie automatically

tags.name: Deprecated
tags.description:
Legacy API endpoints scheduled for removal.

**Important:** These endpoints will be removed on **2025-06-30**.
Please migrate to the v2 equivalents before this date.

| Deprecated Endpoint | Replacement |
|---------------------|-------------|
| `POST /v1/files/upload` | `POST /v2/files/upload` |
| `POST /v1/files` | `POST /v2/files` |

tags.name: File Collections
tags.description:
Organize files into collections (folders) for better management.

Collections can be:
- **User-scoped**: Personal collections visible only to the creating user
- **Global**: Shared collections available to all users for a schema

Collections support hierarchical organization with parent-child relationships.

post.description:
Create pre-signed S3 URL to upload a file to keep temporarily (one week).

Use the saveFileV2 operation to store file file permanently.

parameters.name: file_entity_id
parameters.description:
Use this parameter when uploading a file directly to an existing file entity.

Note: still requires calling saveFileV2 to save the file permanently.

201.description: Pre-signed URL for POST / PUT upload

post.description:
Saves a permanent file entity. Updates an existing file entity when `_id` is passed.

Saves metadata to file entity and stores a version when `s3ref` or `source_url` is passed.

oneOf.description:
Batch version mode: array of s3ref payloads to add multiple versions to a single file entity.
All payloads must target the same file entity (_id or file_entity_id).
Requires version_only=true query parameter.
S3 operations run in parallel, with a single entity update at the end.

New file from s3ref.description: Standard epilot file entity with S3 Ref

New file from source_url.description: New epilot file entity from a source_url

File with custom download URL (external).description: Custom file entity with custom download url

Batch save multiple versions.description: Save multiple file versions in a single request (parallel S3 processing)

post.description:
**DEPRECATED** - Will be removed on **2025-06-30**. Use `POST /v2/files/upload` instead.

## Migration Guide
Replace calls to this endpoint with `uploadFileV2`:

| v1 Parameter | v2 Parameter | Notes |
|--------------|--------------|-------|
| `file_entity_id` | `file_entity_id` | No change |

The v2 response includes the same fields with improved structure.

---

Create pre-signed S3 URL to upload a file to keep temporarily (one week).

Use the saveFile operation to store file file permanently.

parameters.name: file_entity_id
parameters.description: file entity id

201.description: Pre-signed URL for POST / PUT upload

public_url.description: Returned only if file is permanent i.e. file_entity_id is passed

post.description:
**DEPRECATED** - Will be removed on **2025-06-30**. Use `POST /v2/files` instead.

## Migration Guide
Replace calls to this endpoint with `saveFileV2`:

| v1 Feature | v2 Feature | Notes |
|------------|------------|-------|
| `activity_id` param | `activity_id` param | No change |
| `async` param | `async` param | No change |
| - | `fill_activity` param | New in v2 |
| - | `strict` param | New in v2 |
| - | `delete_temp_file` param | New in v2, defaults to true |

The v2 endpoint supports additional parameters for better control over file saving behavior.

---

Create / Update a permanent File entity.

Makes file object permanent and saves metadata to file entity.

get.description: Get a file entity by id

schema.description: Generate a source_url for the file entity, if it doesn't have one

delete.description: Delete a file entity by id

schema.description: Provide `true` to permanently delete the file from storage, otherwise it will be soft-deleted

get.description:
Generate a pre-signed download URL for a file.

The returned URL is valid for a limited time (typically 15 minutes) and can be used to download the file directly.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the file entity

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Index of the file version to download (0 = latest)

parameters.name: attachment
parameters.description: Controls the Content-Disposition header. Set to `true` to trigger browser download dialog, `false` to display inline.

download_url.description: Pre-signed S3 URL valid for downloading the file

post.description:
Generate a pre-signed download URL for a file using its S3 reference.

Use this endpoint when you have the S3 bucket and key but not the file entity ID.

parameters.name: s3_key
parameters.description:
The S3 object key, as returned in `s3ref.key` of the file entity.

Note: object keys store the filename segment percent-encoded. For example, a file named
`Straße 1.pdf` is stored under the key `.../Stra%C3%9Fe%201.pdf`, and that is the value
`s3ref.key` contains. Pass it exactly as returned by the API and make sure your HTTP client
URL-encodes the query parameter value, so a literal `%` arrives encoded as `%25`.

parameters.name: s3_bucket
parameters.description: The S3 bucket name

parameters.name: attachment
parameters.description: Controls the Content-Disposition header. Set to `true` to trigger browser download dialog, `false` to display inline.

download_url.description: Pre-signed S3 URL valid for downloading the file

post.description:
Bulk generate pre-signed download URLs for multiple files in a single request.

This is more efficient than calling `downloadFile` multiple times when you need to download several files.

200.description: Generated download URLs for each requested file

download_url.description: Pre-signed S3 URL for downloading the file

file_entity_id.description: The file entity ID (matches the requested ID)

post.description:
Create a background job to ZIP multiple files and send a download link via email.

For bulk downloads of more than 50 files, use this async endpoint instead of client-side zipping.
The job will:
1. Download all requested files from S3
2. Create a ZIP archive
3. Upload the ZIP to temporary storage
4. Send an email notification with the download link

The ZIP file will be available for 7 days.

get.description: Get the status of a ZIP job

parameters.name: job_id
parameters.description: The UUID of the ZIP job

get.description: Get summary text for a file entity together with the current summary job status when available.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the file entity

post.description: Create or return the current AI summary job for a file entity.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the file entity

get.description: Get the latest AI summary job for the file entity's current source.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the file entity

get.description: Get an AI summary job by id.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the file entity

parameters.name: job_id
parameters.description: The UUID of the summary job

post.description: Compatibility alias for creating or returning the current AI summary job for a file entity.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the file entity

get.description: Get the plain-text representation of a file entity. Returns status `not_ready` while text is being prepared and `unsupported` when text is unavailable for the file or organization.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the file entity

200.description: Text state for the file entity

get.description: Generate a thumbnail preview for a file entity. Supported file types include images (PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP), PDFs, and common document formats. The preview is returned as an image (PNG or JPEG). **Tip:** Use with CookieAuth to embed previews directly in `` tags.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the file entity

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Index of the file version to preview (0 = latest)

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: Desired width in pixels (maintains aspect ratio if only width is specified)

parameters.name: h
parameters.description: Desired height in pixels (maintains aspect ratio if only height is specified)

post.description:
Generate a thumbnail preview from an S3 reference.

Use this endpoint when you have the S3 bucket and key but not the file entity ID.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: Desired width in pixels

parameters.name: h
parameters.description: Desired height in pixels

get.description: Get a thumbnail preview from an S3 reference using query parameters. This GET variant is useful for embedding previews directly in `` tags.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The S3 object key

parameters.name: bucket
parameters.description: The S3 bucket name

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: Desired width in pixels

parameters.name: h
parameters.description: Desired height in pixels

get.description:
Generate a thumbnail preview for a public file entity.

**No authentication required.** This endpoint only works for files with `access_control: public-read`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the public file entity

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Index of the file version to preview (0 = latest)

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: Desired width in pixels

parameters.name: h
parameters.description: Desired height in pixels

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: Organization ID that owns the file

200.description: Generated thumbnail image for a public file

ETag.description: Identifier of the previewed file version, for conditional requests

204.description:
No preview could be generated and no redirect target is available
(the file has no public URL). No body is returned.

302.description:
The preview could not be optimized (e.g. image dimensions above the input
pixel limit, an oversized file, or an unsupported/undecodable format).
Since the file is public, the client is redirected to the original file,
which the browser can render directly.

Location.description: URL of the original public file

304.description:
The preview is unchanged since the version identified by the `If-None-Match`
request header. No body is returned; the cached copy should be reused.

ETag.description: Identifier of the current file version's preview

get.description: Start a browser session by converting a Bearer token into a server-side cookie. **Use case:** After calling this endpoint, you can use preview URLs directly in `` tags without needing to set the Authorization header manually. **Example flow:** 1. Call this endpoint with your Bearer token: `GET /v1/files/session` with `Authorization: Bearer ` 2. The server sets an HTTP-only cookie named `token` 3. Use preview URLs directly: ``

200.description: Session started successfully. A session cookie has been set.

Set-Cookie.description: HTTP-only session cookie containing the authentication token

delete.description:
End a browser session by deleting the token cookie.

Call this endpoint to log out and clear the session cookie.

200.description: Session deleted successfully. The session cookie has been cleared.

post.description:
Generate a public link to share a private file externally.

The generated link:
- Is permanent until explicitly revoked
- Includes the filename for user-friendly URLs
- Does not require authentication to access
- Redirects to a signed download URL when accessed

**Use case:** Share invoices, contracts, or documents with external parties who don't have epilot accounts.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the file entity to share

schema.description: The public URL that can be shared externally

get.description: Fetches all public links previously generated for a file

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the file entity

get.description:
Access a file via its public link.

**No authentication required.** This endpoint redirects to a signed S3 URL for downloading the file.

The filename in the URL is for user-friendliness and SEO; the actual file is identified by the link ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the public link (not the file entity ID)

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The filename (for user-friendly URLs)

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: Optional cache-busting hash to force re-download

302.description: Redirect to a signed S3 URL for downloading the file

Location.description: The signed S3 URL to download the file

404.description: Public link not found or has been revoked

delete.description: Revokes a given public link by ID

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the public link to revoke

post.description:
Verify that a custom download URL is valid and has not expired.

Use this endpoint to validate custom download URLs before redirecting users.
Custom download URLs include a signature and expiration time for security.

valid.description: Whether the URL is valid and not expired

post.description:
Create a pre-signed S3 URL for uploading a file without authentication.

**No authentication required.** This endpoint is intended for public-facing forms and journeys
where end-users need to upload files without logging in.

The uploaded file is stored temporarily (one week). Use `saveFileV2` with proper authentication
to store the file permanently.

**Security note:** Files uploaded via this endpoint are temporary and require authenticated
access to be saved permanently.

201.description: Pre-signed URL for uploading the file

allOf.description: S3 reference to use when saving the file permanently

upload_url.description: Pre-signed URL for uploading the file via PUT request

error.description: Error message if the upload preparation failed

get.description:
Get all file collections for the current user within a specific schema.

Collections help organize files into logical groups (e.g., "Contracts", "Invoices").
User collections are private to the creating user.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: The entity schema slug (e.g., order, opportunity, contact)

200.description: List of collections for the user and schema

post.description:
Create a new file collection for the current user within a specific schema.

The collection will be private to the creating user and associated with the specified schema.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: The entity schema slug (e.g., order, opportunity, contact)

put.description:
Update an existing file collection.

You can update the name, parent relationships, starred status, and enabled locations/purposes.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: The entity schema slug (e.g., order, opportunity)

parameters.name: collectionSlug
parameters.description: The collection slug identifier

delete.description:
Delete a file collection.

**Note:** Deleting a collection does not delete the files within it.
Files will remain but will no longer be associated with this collection.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: The entity schema slug (e.g., order, opportunity)

parameters.name: collectionSlug
parameters.description: The collection slug identifier

get.description:
Get all files within a specific collection for an entity.

The schema is automatically derived from the entity. This endpoint requires
view permission on the parent entity to access its files.

parameters.name: collectionSlug
parameters.description: The collection slug identifier

200.description: List of files in the collection

403.description: Insufficient permissions to view the entity's files

example.error: User must have permission to view this entity to access its files

get.description:
Get all global file collections for a specific schema.

Global collections are shared across all users in the organization for the specified schema.
Unlike user collections, these are visible to everyone with access to entities of that schema.

parameters.name: schemaSlug
parameters.description: The entity schema slug (e.g., order, opportunity, contact)

200.description: List of global collections for the schema

EpilotAuth.description:
Bearer token authentication using epilot OAuth2 JWT tokens.

**When to use:** Server-to-server integrations, API clients, and programmatic access.

**How to obtain a token:**
1. Use the epilot Auth API to authenticate
2. Include the token in the `Authorization` header: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

**Example:**
```
Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
```

**Token contents:** The JWT contains user identity, organization ID, and permissions.

CookieAuth.name: token
CookieAuth.description: Cookie-based session authentication for browser applications. **When to use:** Browser-based applications that need to: - Embed file previews directly in `` tags - Download files without JavaScript token handling - Access files from HTML elements that cannot set custom headers **How to establish a session:** 1. Obtain a Bearer token via EpilotAuth 2. Call `GET /v1/files/session` with the Bearer token 3. The server sets an HTTP-only cookie named `token` 4. Subsequent requests automatically include the cookie **Security note:** The cookie is HTTP-only and secure, protecting against XSS attacks.

EntitySlug.description: URL-friendly identifier for the entity schema

oneOf.description: Empty string (used when file ID not yet assigned)

_manifest.description: Manifest ID used to create/update the entity

etag.description: Content hash (S3 ETag) of the latest file version. For single-part uploads this is the hex MD5 of the content and is stable across orgs, so identical content yields an identical etag. Used to detect real content changes without re-downloading the bytes.

public_url.description: Direct URL for file (public only if file access control is public-read)

preview_summary_de.description: Compact German summary for hover and list preview surfaces.

short_summary_de.description: Short German paragraph summary for file preview surfaces.

preview_summary_en.description: Compact English summary for hover and list preview surfaces.

short_summary_en.description: Short English paragraph summary for file preview surfaces.

FileSummaryJobStatus.description: Current state of a file summary job.

error.description: Human-readable failure or unsupported reason when available.

job_id.description: Current summary job ID when available.

preview_summary_de.description: Compact German summary for hover and list preview surfaces.

short_summary_de.description: Short German paragraph summary for file preview surfaces.

preview_summary_en.description: Compact English summary for hover and list preview surfaces.

short_summary_en.description: Short English paragraph summary for file preview surfaces.

FileTextStatus.description: Availability of the plain-text representation for a file entity.

text.description: Plain-text representation, trimmed and truncated to a server-side limit of 50000 characters.

truncated.description: True when the returned text was cut off by the character limit.

total_chars.description: Length of the full text before truncation.

CustomDownloadUrl.description: Custom external download url used for the file

CustomDownloadUrlAuth.description: Authorization mode for the custom_download_url. `presigned` (the default) uses an HMAC-signed URL; `token` authorizes the download via the caller's bearer token, matched against the exact stored custom_download_url. In token mode the File API returns the unsigned custom_download_url (no expires_at/signature query params) so the stored url matches exactly.

source_url.description: Source URL for the file. Included if the entity was created from source_url, or when ?source_url=true

__additional.description: Additional fields that are not part of the schema

allOf.description: if passed, adds a new version to existing file entity

relations.description: List of entities to relate the file to

BatchSaveFileVersionPayload.description: Payload for batch version save. Only s3ref payloads are supported.

allOf.description: Target file entity to add version to

index_tag.description: Used to index the file at the storage layer, which helps when browsing for this file

metadata.description: Allows passing in custom metadata for the file, expects key-value pairs of string type

public_url.description: Returned only if file is permanent i.e. file_entity_id is passed

CreateZipJobPayload.description:
Request payload to create a ZIP job for bulk file download.
Provide either an explicit list of `file_entity_ids` (up to 1000) or an
`entity_query` that resolves to file entities (up to 10000).

file_entity_ids.description: List of file entity IDs to include in the ZIP

entity_query.description: Entity search query used to resolve files to include in the ZIP

q.description: Lucene-style search query (same syntax as entity search)

sort.description: Sort expression forwarded to the entity search

zip_filename.description: Name of the generated ZIP file

notify_email.description: Email address to notify when the ZIP is ready. Omit to skip the email notification.

job_id.description: Unique identifier for the ZIP job

status.description: Current status of the ZIP job

total_files.description: Total number of files to process

failed_files.description: Number of files that failed to download

result.description: Result of the completed ZIP job

file_entity_id.description: File entity ID of the generated ZIP

zip_size_bytes.description: Size of the generated ZIP file in bytes

error.example: Failed to download file: access denied

created_by.description: User ID who created the job

updated_at.description: When the job was last updated

custom_download_url.description: Custom external download url with signature and expiration time

etag.description: Content hash (S3 ETag) of this file version

last_accessed_at.description: The most recent timestamp when the file was accessed

BaseEntityOwner.description:
The user / organization owning this entity.

Note: Owner implicitly has access to the entity regardless of ACLs.

BaseEntityAcl.description: Access control list (ACL) for an entity. Defines sharing access to external orgs or users.

ErrorObject.description: A generic error returned by the API

status.description: The HTTP status code of the error

FileCollectionId.description: Generated uuid for a file collection

FileCollectionItem.description: A file collection with identifiers and timestamps

slug.description:
Full slug for the collection. Format depends on collection type:
- User collection: `_system_files_collection_{entity_uuid}_{user_id}:{collection_name}`
  Example: `_system_files_collection_3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6_10234:documents`
- Global collection: `_system_files_collection_schema_{schema_slug}:{collection_name}`
  Example: `_system_files_collection_schema_opportunity:templates`

parents.description:
Array of parent collection slugs, empty array if top-level collection. Format depends on collection type:
- User collection: `_system_files_collection_{entity_uuid}_{user_id}`
  Example: `["_system_files_collection_3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6_10234"]`
- Global collection: `_system_files_collection_schema_{schema_slug}`
  Example: `["_system_files_collection_schema_opportunity"]`

starred.description: Whether the collection is starred / favorited

enabled_locations.description: List of location slugs where the collection is enabled. If empty, enabled for all.

enabled_purposes.description: List of purpose slugs where the collection is enabled. If empty, enabled for all.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the collection was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the collection was last updated

parents.description: Array of parent collection slugs, empty array if top-level collection

starred.description: Whether the collection is starred / favorited

enabled_locations.description: List of location slugs where the collection is enabled. If empty, enabled for all.

enabled_purposes.description: List of purpose IDs where the collection is enabled. If empty, enabled for all.

FileCollectionCreateRequest.description: Request body for creating a file collection

StrictQueryParam.name: strict
StrictQueryParam.description: When passed true, the response will contain only fields that match the schema, with non-matching fields included in `__additional`

ActivityIdQueryParam.name: activity_id
ActivityIdQueryParam.description: Activity to include in event feed

FillActivityQueryParam.name: fill_activity
FillActivityQueryParam.description:
Update the diff and entity for the custom activity included in the query.
Pending state on activity is automatically ended when activity is filled.

AsyncOperationQueryParam.name: async
AsyncOperationQueryParam.description: Don't wait for updated entity to become available in Search API. Useful for large migrations

DeleteTempFileQueryParam.name: delete_temp_file
DeleteTempFileQueryParam.description: Delete the temp file from S3 after copying it permanently

VersionOnlyQueryParam.name: version_only
VersionOnlyQueryParam.description:
When true, only adds a new file version and updates the entity's
s3ref to point to the new version, without overwriting the entity's
existing top-level metadata. The entity's filename, type, and other
fields are preserved as-is. The new version entry in the versions
array will contain the file-level metadata (filename, mime_type, etc).
Only applies when updating an existing entity (_id or file_entity_id is set).

ForbiddenError.description: Insufficient permissions to access the resource

example.error: Forbidden: You do not have permission to access this file

NotFoundError.description: The requested resource was not found

example.error: Not Found: File entity not found

ConflictError.description: The request conflicts with the current resource state

example.error: Extracted file content is still being prepared

InternalServerError.description: An unexpected error occurred on the server
